items.headline: Spain vs. France semifinal is locked in — and two more quarterfinals go tonight
items.body: The last four is taking shape. Spain edged Belgium 2-1 on Friday, settled once again by a late Mikel Merino goal after a sub keeper spilled a routine shot — that came a day after France dispatched Cinderella side Morocco 2-0. So it's Spain vs. France in one semifinal, a rematch of Euro 2024. The bracket finishes filling in tonight: Haaland's Norway take on England (5pm ET), then Messi's Argentina — fresh off a wild comeback run — face stubborn Switzerland (8pm ET).
items.say: World Cup's down to the wire — Spain and France are through to the semis, and tonight it's Haaland's Norway vs. England, then Messi against Switzerland. Who do you like to make the final?

links.text: Yahoo Sports: quarterfinals live — Spain-France semi set
Al Jazeera: today's quarterfinals — schedule & previews

items.headline: Sinner dismisses Djokovic and books a title rematch — plus a historic all-Czech women's final
items.body: It's finals weekend at the All England Club. World No. 1 Jannik Sinner powered past 39-year-old Novak Djokovic in straight sets, 6-4, 6-4, 6-4, barely blinking on serve, to reach Sunday's final against Alexander Zverev — who's chasing the rare 'Channel Slam' after winning the French Open. And on the women's side, Wimbledon is guaranteed a brand-new champion today: it's the first-ever all-Czech Grand Slam final, Karolina Muchova vs. Linda Noskova, with neither having won a major before.
items.say: Wimbledon finals are here — Sinner destroyed Djokovic to set up a final with Zverev on Sunday. And the women's final today is two Czechs, both going for their first-ever Slam. Good weekend for tennis.

links.text: ESPN: Sinner beats Djokovic, will face Zverev for the title

items.headline: The Greek Freak is heading to South Beach — Giannis traded to the Heat
items.body: The domino everyone was waiting on finally fell. After 14 years and a title in Milwaukee, two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo is being traded to the Miami Heat (along with Bobby Portis) for a big haul — Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis and a stack of first-round picks. It pairs Giannis with defensive anchor Bam Adebayo and instantly makes Miami a contender: their title odds jumped almost overnight. It's been an absolutely wild NBA offseason, and this is the biggest one yet.
items.say: Giannis got traded to the Heat — after 14 years in Milwaukee. Him and Bam Adebayo together is scary. This NBA offseason has been total chaos, and that's the biggest domino of them all.

links.text: NBA.com: Heat acquire Giannis in blockbuster deal

items.headline: Hometown hero Bob MacIntyre is out front at the Scottish Open
items.body: A feel-good leaderboard in Scotland. Local favorite Robert 'Bob' MacIntyre — the pride of Oban — is leading the Genesis Scottish Open at The Renaissance Club, thrilling the home crowd as he chases the title on his own soil. It's also the perfect tune-up: the year's final major, The Open Championship, tees off next week, and a hot links form is exactly what you want heading in. Great lazy-weekend golf to have on in the background.
items.say: The Scottish guy Bob MacIntyre is leading the Scottish Open on home soil right now — crowd's loving it. Nice warm-up too, since The Open, the last major, is next week. Easy weekend watch.

links.text: CBS Sports: 2026 Genesis Scottish Open leaderboard

items.headline: Merlier makes it two — back-to-back sprint wins as Pogačar cruises in yellow
items.body: The fast men had their say again. Belgian speedster Tim Merlier timed his kick to perfection in Bergerac to win Stage 8, his second straight bunch-sprint victory of this Tour, after a brave day-long breakaway got reeled in inside the final couple of kilometers. Nothing changed at the top: Tadej Pogačar comfortably kept the yellow jersey on a flat day made for the sprinters. A nice, low-stakes one to have on before the mountains bite again.
items.say: The Tour had another sprint finish — this Belgian guy Merlier won again, two in a row now. Pogačar's still comfortably in yellow. Perfect background watching for a lazy Saturday.

links.text: Cyclingnews: Stage 8 live — another bunch sprint in Bergerac

items.headline: Disney's live-action 'Moana' opens soft — a $40M-ish debut and rough reviews
items.body: The remake wave hit a wave of its own. Disney's live-action 'Moana' — with Dwayne Johnson back as Maui — is still expected to top the weekend box office, but the opening number came in light at roughly $40-45M domestically, well below the buzz for a movie with a reported $250M budget. Critics haven't been kind either (a chilly 36% on Rotten Tomatoes). It'll still be the family go-to this weekend — just not the runaway smash Disney hoped for. Cue the 'are we over the remakes?' debate.
items.say: The live-action Moana came out and… kind of flopped — soft opening, rough reviews. Still the kid-friendly pick this weekend, but people are asking if we're all done with these Disney remakes. You over them yet?

items.headline: Teddy Swims drops 'Break Up in Reverse' — and the video is a stunner
items.body: The 'Lose Control' guy is back with a gut-punch. Teddy Swims released 'Break Up in Reverse' this week — a soulful, aching breakup song that isn't about bitterness so much as wishing you could rewind and do it all again. The video is the talking point: it opens on a pile of rubble that a bulldozer slowly reassembles, in reverse, into a warm, memory-filled room. It's the kind of big-voiced ballad that'll be everywhere on the radio, and a solid pick for the drive home.
items.say: Teddy Swims — the huge-voiced 'Lose Control' guy — put out a new song, 'Break Up in Reverse.' The video's really something, this whole rewinding-a-breakup idea. Give it a listen.

links.text: 'Break Up in Reverse' — Official Music Video (Teddy Swims)
Rolling Stone: Teddy Swims reflects on heartbreak on new single

items.headline: 'Murder 101' — the true-crime docuseries everyone will be talking about — hits Prime Monday
items.body: Your next weekend binge is nearly here. 'Murder 101,' a Prime Video docuseries based on the hit true-crime podcast, drops all three episodes Monday, July 13. The hook is a great one: a high-school teacher and his students in small-town Tennessee take on a real cold case — the decades-old 'Redhead Murders' — as an actual class project, and start turning up leads that stumped the grown-ups. It's from some of the 'Spider-Man' movie producers, and it's built for a one-sitting watch.
items.say: There's a true-crime show coming to Prime Monday, Murder 101 — high schoolers actually reinvestigating a real cold case as a class project. Sounds wild. Perfect if you love that true-crime stuff.

items.headline: Suki Waterhouse's 'Loveland' is out — and the 'Notting Hill' video is a charmer
items.body: The actress-turned-real-musician keeps leveling up. Suki Waterhouse released her new album 'Loveland' this week — 14 dreamy tracks made with an all-star cast of collaborators, including The National's Aaron Dessner and even Fleetwood Mac's Mick Fleetwood. The lead video, 'Notting Hill,' is a warm love letter to her old London neighborhood, complete with a cheeky nod to the 1999 Julia Roberts movie. A breezy, easy-listening pick — and a fun 'did you know she's a musician now?' at the water cooler.
items.say: Did you know Suki Waterhouse — she's an actress — has a legit music career now? New album's out, and the 'Notting Hill' video is really pretty, little nod to the old Julia Roberts movie. Easy summer listen.

links.text: Universal Music: Suki Waterhouse releases new album 'Loveland'
